description: Stream Live IPTV, Video On-Demand, and Series from an Xtream-compatible server using this plugin.
name: Jellyfin Xtream
overview: Stream content from an Xtream-compatible server.

versions.changelog:
Growing TS stream endpoint for continuous recording playback

- Recordings now use a .strm file pointing to a growing TS stream endpoint
- Stream continues growing while watching (no more termination at playback start)
- Fixed cleanup order and merge-failure handling
- Improved file drain logic and reduced poll interval
- Windows compatibility fix (FileShare.Delete)

## Changes

- **Fix**: Drop .strm approach (caused Jellyfin to transcode HLS→AV1, breaking web)
- **Dual output**: ffmpeg writes both .ts file (library) + HLS segments (API) simultaneously
- .ts file = Jellyfin serves directly → works on all clients (iPhone, Android TV, web)
- HLS API = seekable growing playback at `/Xtream/Recordings/{timerId}/stream.m3u8`
- After recording: merges HLS→MKV, deletes .ts and segments

## Playback during recording
- **From Jellyfin library**: plays the growing .ts file (all clients)
- **Via HLS API**: seekable, continuously growing stream

## Changes

- Record to HLS format (m3u8 + ts segments) for seekable, growing in-progress playback
- New API endpoints for HLS streaming
- .strm file created during recording for Jellyfin library integration
- HLS segments merged to MKV after recording completes
- Cleanup of orphaned recording artifacts on startup
- Works on iPhone, Android TV, and web

## Changes

- Stop registering recordings with Jellyfin's internal active recordings tracker
- This prevents Jellyfin from treating in-progress recordings as infinite live streams
- Recordings are now served as normal video files — seekable, direct-playable, compatible with Android TV
- MPEG-TS format ensures the file is readable while still being written

## Changes

- Record to MPEG-TS (.ts) instead of MKV for better in-progress playback and seeking
- Add -sn to exclude subtitle streams (unsupported in MPEG-TS)
- Remux to MKV after recording completes for better library support
- Trigger media library scan when a recording starts and finishes so new recordings appear promptly

## Changes
- Fix recording playback on Android TV
- Switch to fragmented MP4 (from MKV) for active recording playback
- Stream copy instead of re-encoding (zero CPU, instant availability)
- Add aac_adtstoasc bitstream filter for MPEG-TS compatibility
- Fix library scan trigger timing
- Better Android/iOS seeking with default_base_moof flag

## Changes
- Searchable XMLTV channel input with datalist autocomplete
- Auto-match XMLTV channels by name similarity
- External XMLTV EPG source support with per-channel mapping
- EPG Sources management UI
- Improved overrides page styling and readability
- Fix XmltvChannelId persistence
- SSL and gzip support for XMLTV fetches

- Recordings now use ffmpeg MKV remux for seekable in-progress recordings
- Fix catchup channel timezone display
- Fix catchup date filter with timezone shift
- Cache invalidation on timezone config change
- Recording engine, timer persistence, active recordings UI
- Per-channel EPG timezone support
